rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M2+ rule; this is an M1 exercise. The compound prompt's long-read callout even sets up steer-and-continue, but trust-distance ramp is not yet in scope.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: 50% magic rule (M3-M8)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Scoped to M3-M8 magic distribution; M1 exercise out of scope.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust the agentic design — no human backstops
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No human-backstop rulesets on agent flows; Claude is given authority to review the session and write the file. The confirmation kept (student ships the ticket update / pastes manually) is the legitimate user-visible/trust-line exception the rule carves out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Quality ladder
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Quality-tier scoring (7/8/9) is a maintainer authoring judgment, not a static body check; no ladder defect visible in the file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: One exercise = one bounded activity
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Bounded activity = close the loop outside the repo on the shipped bug (compound the session, then reach out to the tracker). The compound + MCP + close-ticket beats chain mechanically on the same bug/session without sub-numbering; internal arc (do then push back then sweep) stays one exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Modules composable across variants
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Shared-library / VARIANT-marker composability is a cross-file authoring concern; no fork present in this single file to evaluate.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Three-level completeness
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Strategy frame (The point) -> runtime artifact (maintainer block: watch-fors, decision points, leap test the trainer reads at a blocker) -> sendable asset (the ticket close-out + CLAUDE.local.md the student produces). Chain reads backward cleanly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pattern-recognition LOs need n>=2 instances with contrast
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Bloom LO is Apply (wire connector, close ticket) + Analyze (read retro against session moments) — not a read/spot/judge pattern-recognition verb requiring n>=2 exposures. No un-met pattern LO.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Progression-with-variations is the spine (9b)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The compound move recurs across modules under variation; the maintainer block names the new face for M1 (compound born from this session's evidence, extended by every module after) and the arc hands off to M2. Not verbatim repetition of a later compound beat.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Agency is explicit and respected: 'What you do with it is your call' on the gitignore edit; three close-out paths the student picks among; no forced pacing or 'sit N minutes' compulsion.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Delegation — student brings, Claude screens
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Student brings the ticket (pastes ID/URL or says create-new) and decides the path; Claude screens/drafts (reads ticket, writes close-out). Tracker access is named via connector/gh with a manual-paste fallback, so no inaccessible-surface delegation. Picking the tracker stays the student's call.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Split mega-exercise when phases = distinct skills + distinct artifacts
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Compound and close-the-ticket are different skills/artifacts but chain mechanically on the same session/bug with no hard cognitive break demanding a separate exercise; the counter (phases chain mechanically, stays one) applies. Not a split candidate.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Named entities are real/accessible: CLAUDE.local.md (created in-prompt), gh (auth-checked in-session), MCP connector (install line + /mcp + claude --resume recovery named), referenced reference pages exist under reference/ and supplementary/. No 'no' answers.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teach principle + technique — default
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Not a black-box wow module. The point names WHY (session is the evidence, not a retro interview) and the MCP section teaches WHY the agent must reach outside the repo plus the connector/action/tool vocabulary (HOW). No magic invocation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Auto-fire sim+eval after significant rewrite
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Process rule for the generation cycle (dispatch sim+eval before a done summary), not a static property of the file body; this judge is the eval, no body defect to score.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go; live in prompts not body prose
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forcing functions live in the four fenced prompts (review session and write the file; read the ticket; update and report; sweep-and-integrate). Read prompt-only, the mood/artifact still land. Body callouts are optional push-backs / recovery / timebox — within the carve-out. No count-scripts ('push back twice') in fences.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Corporate infra = nice-to-have, never forcing function
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: MCP/tracker connector is framed as enhancement; path 3 (manual paste of the Claude-written close-out) delivers the same teaching moment (agent writes the real note; student lands it). Personal/gh path is a first-class option, not a 'fallback' apology.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Personal-first, team promotion via human conversation
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: CLAUDE.local.md is the personal gitignored file; the compound prompt explicitly flags team-worthy rules in the summary (not auto-PR'd) and leaves the team ./CLAUDE.md PR to the student's decision. Agent does not unilaterally change shared infra.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Measure silent failure, not just friction
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cohort-evaluation heuristic (two-question pass-rate framing), not a static body check; no in-file artifact to verdict.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't pre-set categories before experiment runs
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Evaluation-design discipline (no big-design-up-front filters before data lands); not a property of this exercise body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification layers sample DIFFERENT error distributions
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The exercise's own verification is multi-layer and distinct-shape: student reads the summary and pushes back (mood/correctness), verifies against the file/scrollback, ships the ticket update. No single-layer 'done' claim in the body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cheap-before-expensive verification ordering (21b)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cheap-first ordering honored: read-the-summary push-back precedes shipping; the long-read callout steers narrower (cheap interrupt) before any expensive re-run. No expensive verification stacked on something failing a cheap check.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rule in memory without forcing function = worse than no rule
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Governs compounding a memory rule (name its forcing-function location); not a property of this student-facing exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Short skeptical pushback on done = re-verify trigger
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Author/agent self-discipline on done-signals during generation; not an in-file property to verdict.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cohort execution is trainer-paced + concurrent
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Time budget (30 min inside M1's 2h slot) is consistent with concurrent trainer-paced delivery; the long agent reads (session review, MCP install) are natural pause anchors, not summed sequential math. No sequential-overrun framing in the body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Host-skill optional; maintainer blocks trainer-only
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Push-backs are delivered by the trainer from the maintainer block ('Push-back moves at retro and MCP gate (trainer delivers)'); no host-skill (Nerd/Teacher) named as a cohort prereq, no 'Trainer / Nerd' dual-actor cells.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats: in-chat over file-write
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The reflection move (push back, quote the moment) is in-chat. The file write that exists — CLAUDE.local.md — is a legitimate downstream-read artifact (used throughout the training), not a proof-of-reflection comfort blanket. No reflection-only file write.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Classroom = default delivery; body teaches work, trainer manages room
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body authors no group/pair/room-share, no pause-cues, no 'discuss with'. Sentences are about the work (artifact, prompt, next action); cohort-management push-backs live in the maintainer block, not the body. Long agent reads serve as natural unauthored pauses.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Eval = fixed yardstick; thing measured learns
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Scorer-immutability discipline for score-trajectory loops; no eval/scorer loop inside this exercise to evaluate.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reading burden for verification = banned; Claude audits Claude
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Student reads the retro summary and close-out for emotional/voice payoff (does it sound like our team; quote the moment) — the allowed reason — not to scan Claude's output for correctness errors. No Claude-on-Claude reading burden offloaded to the student. (M1 foreshadows; M5/M6 own Claude-on-Claude verification.)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Self-report + self-audit charity — verify at artifact, over-flag or fresh-session
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Self-report inflation is mitigated: the student is told to read the summary and push back, quoting the session moment, rather than ending the phase on Claude's account. The maintainer watch-for ('quote the specific session moment that made you add rule X; if you can't, take it out') is the copyable push-back the rule asks for. Phase does not end on the summary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Offered structures need forced-engagement mechanic
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The compound prompt offers a structured rewrite of CLAUDE.local.md. The forced-engagement step (push back, quote the moment back at Claude) lives in body prose immediately below the fence. The maintainer block logs this as accept-with-mitigation (§32 engagement in body keeps the fence copy-paste-clean; §50 callout covers self-charity). Accepted-by-design, not a silent gap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-type blindness on ingestion
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Heterogeneous slide/PPTX ingestion failure mode; this exercise ingests a chat session and a ticket, not a mixed-format source folder.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Task selection drives outcome — name floor AND ceiling
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Task-sizing anti-pattern callout ('not an epic') belongs to the send-off task-selection exercise; this exercise inherits the already-shipped bug from fix-tests-first, no task selection here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Maintainer-block hedge naming section's own failure mode = cut-signal (34b)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer watch-fors (retro confabulation, MCP install gate) are recoverable-by-body-callout failure modes, not hedges holding up a speculative section premise. Premise-test passes: removing the hedge leaves the section defensible. No cut signal.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audience tier determines authoring substrate
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 engineer tier: substrate is plain prompts + multi-layer CLAUDE.md (personal CLAUDE.local.md) + MCP connector install — all Day-1 installable for an engineer without stealing the teaching moment (gh fallback, manual-paste path). Right tier.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs smoke-test before trust
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Applies to building hooks/judges/loops (build then smoke-test); this exercise builds no verification machinery. The MCP /mcp status check is a connector liveness check, not eval machinery.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers as staged progression
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Auto/lazy/explicit context-tier reveal is a different module's beat; this exercise teaches compound + MCP reach, not context-economy surfaces.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Removals are strategic — ask before cutting
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Generation-step discipline (pause and ask before cutting a named entity); not a static property of the shipped file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Symmetric visible-mechanics > runtime fork when dual-runtime constraints break agentic move
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Dual-runtime fork-vs-symmetric decision is Agents-101 (CLI+Desktop+Cowork); AE101 prompts are single-runtime (Claude Code). No runtime fork in this file to collapse.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Load-bearing platform feature = no fallback; gate or redesign
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: MCP is NOT the load-bearing move — the teaching moment is 'the agent reaches across a tool boundary with a real engineering note', which path 3 (gh or manual paste of Claude's close-out) delivers without dilution. So the manual path is a legitimate same-lesson redesign, not a politeness fallback that strips the point. Within bounds.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Eval architecture — partition by evidence source
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Judge/sim architecture rule (partition by evidence source, read-only verdicts); governs the eval system, not this exercise body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Plug points + UX
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Plug points name concrete KINDs (sponsor-stated tracker: Linear/Jira/GitHub Issues) and the close-the-ticket phase is structurally downstream — the student's actual tracker changes the connector/path the next agent move takes, so absence of org context dilutes the exercise. UX: prompts are single copy-paste each, paths discoverable, hand-off from fix-tests-first named. No nominal plug point.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — 3 observable Monday outcomes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Three falsifiable Monday outcomes present (compounds one session-derived rule into CLAUDE.local.md; closes a ticket via connector/gh; quotes the session moment that earned a rule before it lands). Each names an observable verb + falsifiable evidence, not a competency claim.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block carries a five-row table mapping each forcing function to its dominant failure mode and one escape hatch (retro confabulation->trainer quote-the-moment push; install gate->tenant-admin fallback / path 3; rubber-stamp->read-aloud push). Both columns present per forcing function.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get body recovery callout
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The compound prompt is a heavy read-the-world-session run; the body callout above it ('Long session, long read ... interrupt with Esc, narrow to orient/introspect, say continue') names the heavy run and the steer-narrower recovery move. Recovery callout present where fan-out warrants it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Save belongs with authoring, not loading
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: CLAUDE.local.md is created/saved at the moment it is authored (the compound prompt writes the file in place), not deferred to a later use exercise. No author-here / save-later asymmetry, no per-runtime save split (single AE101 runtime).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias -> body acknowledgment callout
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The compound prompt accepts self-charity (Claude grades its own session) for single-paste convenience; the body below names the push-back move and the maintainer Accept-with-mitigation block logs all three §50 beats (name the bias, acknowledge the convenience, offer the harsher 'quote the moment / read each rule aloud' alternative). Accepted-with-mitigation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Deliberate-antipattern prompts = class-judge exempt, flagged-by-design
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No deliberate before-shot antipattern prompt here (no intentionally-thin baseline for later contrast). The §32/§50 self-charity items are accepted-with-mitigation (§50), not deliberate-antipattern (§51); the maintainer block files them under the correct rule. Nothing mis-logged.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Session widget — Session verb-name shape; place by learning flow
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: This is the third exercise on the same bug/session/repo; it continues the M1 session rather than starting/forking a new one (the close even names claude --resume <session-id> to stay in the same session), so no Session widget is owed here. Correct placement-by-flow: the widget lived at the module/first-exercise open.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Widgets carry semantics, not just emphasis (52b)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The must-not-be-missed beats use blockquote Note-style callouts (long-read steer, connector-resume, timebox) — guard-an-imminent-mistake / don't-miss meaning — not a misappropriated Session or HOX chip grabbed for loudness. Widget meaning matches beat meaning.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing doesn't preview prompt's payoff
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Pre-action lead-ins name activity/artifact/constraint ('Ask Claude to read the ticket and report what's on it'; 'Ask Claude to update the ticket and report what it wrote'), not what the prompt will reveal. The top 'What you do' names the activity; 'What happened' sits POST-action at the close. No payoff pre-announcement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout, not forcing function
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 engineer audience: the recovery/risk fixes here are body callouts (long-read steer, timebox note, connector-resume aside, push-back-if-stiff) — recoverable in <1 min by the engineer. No new gating prompt added for a recoverable miss; correct default fix-shape.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-session (any new mechanic mid-arc) ships as packaging, not new primitive
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: MCP/connector is the one new primitive this exercise introduces and it is taught as the module's beat, not a smuggled mid-arc mechanic. No multi-session container bolted on; primitive budget respected (compound reuses M1's CLAUDE.local.md primitive from fix-tests-first).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: /clear = tool, not ceremony
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Close handles /clear as a tool, not ritual: the convergence prompt (sweep for anything earned, integrate) fires first, then 'You can now clear' is framed as optional ('may or may not help ... let's find out'), not a mandated closing ceremony. Two-question diagnostic satisfied.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inbound links preserve pedagogical beat across reference restructuring
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-restructuring audit (preserve the beat when renaming/merging a ref page); no reference page is being restructured in this edit. Outbound links to mcp-and-connectors.md / the-agent-loop.md are intact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Scan at the unit the rule names, not convenient larger one
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Judge-process discipline (scan at the rule's named atom); a directive to this judge, not a property of the exercise body. Honored in this ledger by scanning phases/prompts individually.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify mechanism before proposing surgery
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Governs proposing SKILL.md/framework changes (walk the happy-path trace first); not a property of this student-facing exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Callouts next to prompts: steer, don't pre-empt
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Callouts teach a generalizable steering move rather than pinning one ghost failure: the long-read callout is notice-stall -> interrupt -> narrow -> continue; 'push back if stiff' is a recognize-and-loosen move. These survive prompt-mechanic changes; not pre-emption-shaped rot.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Persistent TODO surfaces stale by default — audit before acting
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Punchlist-staleness discipline (verify before acting on pre-cohort-todos / backlog bullets); not a property of this exercise body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Maintainer blocks point, don't restate
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block carries file-unique content (themes, frameworks/sources with URLs, watch-fors, decision points, leap test, per-phase table, accept-with-mitigation). Delivery mechanics (MCP install gate, tracker fallback) point at reference/mcp-and-connectors.md rather than restating it. No cross-cutting paraphrase to rot.
